body_txt: When angry or outraged, a cross- or Y-shaped intersection of veins never fails to appear on an anime character's forehead and/or fist. Occasionally, if the character gets mad enough, extra popping veins will appear in the air around them. Over time, the veins have begun to turn into a stylized symbol like the Sweat Drop , and as seen in the center image may float out away from the character's body. Also, there are absolutely no regulations when it comes to the size of it. It's effective in demonstrating the character is angry despite what other emotion they may be displaying, showing the character is putting on a facade to hide their anger. In certain instances, the veins can be a precursor to a Big Ball of Violence . Usually limited to comedic anime, but some manga have started putting them in speech bubbles , and they have also appeared in anime-influenced Western Animation . Compare Cartoon Throbbing and Frustrated Overhead Scribble . A mild form of Literally Apoplectic , to the extent that it suggests high blood pressure. Sub-Trope of Gestures of Annoyance . See also Briffits and Squeans for various visual mood shorthands that evolved in Western newspaper comics; equivalents to this trope there fall mostly in the broad category of "emanata". While Euro-American and Japanese visual shorthands originally developed in distinct creative environments, it's common for modern visual media to include elements from both systems.
